meta.enrichment_note: Deterministic verdicts preserved verbatim (0 findings, 0 dropped). Bare abstains converted to reasoned assessments; recomputable numbers (c1,c11,c19,c27 thermo/SEI/vib-quanta) recomputed clean before any advisory.

routed_to_human.note: Thermodynamics recomputed: on the N2+3H2->2NH3 basis, 2x NIST dGf(NH3,298)=2*(-16.4)=-32.8 kJ/mol matches the reported value exactly, and is consistent with c27's dH=-91.8 (=2*dHf). Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
Recomputed: 1 eV / (N2 vibrational quantum ~0.2925 eV, omega_e~2359 cm^-1) = 3.42 quanta, consistent with the hedged 'roughly three'. Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the N2 dissociation
barrier is reduced by 1 eV, corresponding to roughly three N2 vibrational quanta.

routed_to_human.note: SEI recomputed deterministically: P/flow at 40 SCCM gives 5/10/15 W -> 7.5/15.0/22.5 kJ/L, matching the reported specific energy inputs exactly. Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
Reaction performance was evaluated at plasma powers of 5, 10, and 15 W,
corresponding to specific energy inputs of 7.5, 15, and 22.5 kJ L−1.

routed_to_human.note: Thermodynamics recomputed: -91.8 kJ/mol = 2x NIST dHf(NH3,298)=2*(-45.9) on the 2-mol-NH3 basis, internally consistent with c1's dG=-32.8. Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote: The ammonia synthesis reaction was modeled with ∆H (298 K) = −91.8 kJ/mol.

routed_to_human.note: Headline T4 claim that NH3 yields EXCEED bulk thermodynamic equilibrium. Extraordinary by chemistry standards (apparent violation of equilibrium is really a non-equilibrium plasma-driven steady state). The verdict rests entirely on the microkinetic model's 1 eV barrier-reduction assumption (c11) and the chosen equilibrium reference. CONFIRM: re-derive the equilibrium NH3 partial pressure at the stated T,P and show the model's predicted P_NH3 strictly exceeds it under the same conditions; check the claim is scoped to 'bulk thermodynamic equilibrium of the unexcited gas', not a true 2nd-law violation.
routed_to_human.quote:
NH3 yields are predicted
to exceed bulk thermodynamic equilibrium limits on materials that are thermal-rate-limited by N2
dissociation.

routed_to_human.note: Central experimental T4 causal claim: beyond-equilibrium NH3 observed and greater on weak-N-binding Pt that is thermally inactive. Physically plausible for plasma activation, but 'exceeding the bulk equilibrium limit' depends on which equilibrium curve is overlaid in Fig 5a and on GC calibration. CONFIRM: validate the plotted equilibrium reference line and verify the measured Pt yield sits above it with the n=3 error bars (c32) not crossing the line.
routed_to_human.quote:
NH3 yields exceeding the
bulk equilibrium limit are observed and are greater on materials with weaker N binding en-
ergies (e.g. Pt) that are inactive for thermal ammonia synthesis.

routed_to_human.note: Causal claim that yields extend further past equilibrium with plasma power and re-equilibrate at high T. Direction is consistent with the model, but 'further beyond equilibrium' is a comparative read across power curves. CONFIRM: monotonic ordering of yield vs power (5/10/15 W) at fixed T below the thermal-dominant window, and convergence to the equilibrium line at high T.
routed_to_human.quote:
with increasing
plasma power, ammonia yields extend further beyond the equilibrium limit, while yields
re-equilibrate at temperatures at which thermal chemistry becomes dominant.

routed_to_human.note: Pt yields 'significantly exceeding the bulk equilibrium limit' and enhancement until ~1000 K are read off Fig 5a. Weakest link: the equilibrium baseline and the ~1000 K convergence point are figure-extracted, not tabulated. CONFIRM: digitize Fig 5a, overlay the computed equilibrium curve, and confirm the exceedance and the ~1000 K crossover quantitatively.
routed_to_human.quote:
Pt continued to enhance NH3 production over the background
at higher temperatures until about 1000 K, after which the yields converged.

routed_to_human.note: Volcano-top optimality of EN=-0.6 eV (intermediate binder highest yield of the three) is a microkinetic-model output read from Fig 2/inset. Appropriate within Sabatier/volcano framing, but optimum position depends on the chosen scaling relations and rate constants. CONFIRM: reproduce the volcano with the stated 473 K/1 atm/5% conversion and show -0.6 eV is the maximum.
routed_to_human.quote:
N with intermediate strength (EN = −0.6 eV), sits at the top of the volcano curve (see inset)
and provides the optimal balance between N2 activation and its subsequent hydrogenation.

routed_to_human.note: Beyond-equilibrium for the optimal catalyst at kene=1e-1 s^-1 over 475-600 K is a figure-read threshold from Fig 3b. CONFIRM: extract the Fig 3b curve and verify the T-window and kene at which the model output crosses the equilibrium line.
routed_to_human.quote:
NH3 yields
exceeding equilibrium for the optimal thermal catalyst are observed at kene = 10−1s
−1
, in
the temperature window of 475–600 K.

routed_to_human.note: Enhancement-over-thermal threshold kene>=1e-2 s^-1 for both materials (Fig 3b,c). Threshold is read from model curves and is sensitive to the plasma-activation rate parameterization. CONFIRM: reproduce the crossover kene for both EN=-0.6 and 0.0 eV.
routed_to_human.quote:
plasma-on models predict enhancements in NH3 produced over thermal catalysis for both
materials if the rate of plasma-activation of N2 is sufficiently high (kene ≥ 10−2s
−1
)

routed_to_human.note: Weak binder (EN=0.0 eV) predicted beyond equilibrium for kene>=1e-2 s^-1 (Fig 3c). Same model-threshold dependence as c14. CONFIRM: extract Fig 3c and verify the beyond-equilibrium onset kene.
routed_to_human.quote:
NH3 pressures are
predicted to go beyond the equilibrium limit for kene ≥ 10−2s
−1
.

routed_to_human.note: Background NH3 yield maximizing at ~920 K (Fig 4) is a figure-extracted optimum. CONFIRM: digitize Fig 4 at 5/10/15 W and confirm the maximum location; a code verifier could recompute it from the underlying yield-vs-T data if released.
routed_to_human.quote:
At all plasma powers, NH3 yields maximized at about 920 K, after which they
decreased.

routed_to_human.note: Light-off temperatures (Ni ~750 K; Pt and bare Al2O3 ~900 K, Fig 5b) are read off decomposition curves. CONFIRM: digitize Fig 5b and confirm the light-off (e.g. 10%-conversion) temperatures; the Pt~=Al2O3 near-identity implies Pt is decomposition-inactive, supporting the catalyst-selection narrative.
routed_to_human.quote:
Ni/Al2O3 is the most active for NH3
decomposition, lighting off at around 750 K. The decomposition curves for Pt/γ-Al2O3 and
metal-free γ-Al2O3 are nearly identical, lighting off at approximately 900 K.

routed_to_human.note: Method note: each datum is mean of n=3 GC injections with SD error bars (T3). n=3 is a thin statistical basis and SD of 3 injections captures only short-term GC repeatability, not run-to-run or catalyst-batch variability. CONFIRM: report whether replicates are independent runs vs injections, and consider whether the beyond-equilibrium exceedances (c24, c33) survive the measurement uncertainty.
routed_to_human.quote:
Each data point is based off
of an average of three injections by the GC and the error bars shown correspond to the
standard deviation of these three measurements.

routed_to_human.note: Prior-work T5 claim (elevated site-time yields at atm/438 K and a shift in optimal catalyst, refs [16,18]). Consistent with this group's DBD plasma-catalysis line of work, but rests on cited literature not reproduced here. CONFIRM: check the cited sources report these conditions and the catalyst-optimum shift as stated.
routed_to_human.quote:
showed elevated site-time yields of NH3 at atmospheric pressure
and 438 K, as well as a shift in optimal catalyst material

routed_to_human.note: N-binding order Ru>Ni>Pt (T5). Qualitatively consistent with the transition-metal N* adsorption trend (earlier/left TMs bind N more strongly; Ru>Ni>Pt is standard surface-science ordering), so the catalyst-selection rationale is sound. CONFIRM against tabulated EN or DFT adsorption energies in the cited references for the exact ordering.
routed_to_human.quote: the N-metal bond strength follows the order Ru > Ni > Pt

routed_to_human.note: Framing claim that plasma catalysis enables 'milder' ammonia-synthesis conditions (~473 K, 1 atm). The conditions themselves are a literature fact (better treated as the c-cluster of transcribed facts), but whether 'milder' constitutes a significant/important advance over Haber-Bosch is a value judgment about the contribution's worth, not a checkable proposition. Route to human.
routed_to_human.quote:
ammonia synthesis can be performed at milder temperatures and pressures (∼473 K, 1 atm) when catalysts are operated alongside non-thermal plas-
mas
